calendar.matters:
Plaintiff'S Motion To Tax Costs Sought By Defendant Paccar, Inc.
Off Calendar Sep-27-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for hearing on plaintiff Billy S. Johnson's Motion to Tax Costs.
Dept. 611, Judge: James J. McBride, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Joe Vickstein, CSR #4780.

Carlos Guzman, Esq. appeared for plaintiff and moving party Billy S. Johnson, who was not present at today's hearing.
Madeline L. Buty, Esq. and Drexwell M. Jones, Esq. appeared for defendant PACCAR, Inc.

After hearing argument by counsel, and good cause appearing, the Court rules as follows:

Plaintiff Billy S. Johnson's motion to tax costs is GRANTED as it pertains to the filing fees for the motions to continue trial in the amount of $80.00. In all other aspects the motion is DENIED. Defendant PACCAR, Inc. is awarded costs in the amount of $90,899.68.

Order was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Notice Of Motion To Tax Cost Sought By Defendant Carlisle Corporation
Off Calendar Jul-28-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion to tax costs sought by defendant Carlisle Corporation is granted in part and denied in part. Of the $1570.20 sought in the costs memo for filing and motion fees, $112.50 is taxed because it was not included in the costs worksheet and another $70 is taxed (included in the Lexis-Nexis filing fees) because there is insufficient description for what those costs were for and thus the court cannot determine whether those costs were reasonably necessary. Of the $13,653.32 stated in the costs worksheet for deposition costs, the following items are taxed because there is no explanation in defendant's papers who the deponents are and thus the court cannot determine whether the costs for those depositions were reasonably necessary: $545 for the deposition of Michael Ellenbecker, $965.95 for the deposition of Manuel Joaquin, $831,50 for the deposition of Mark Taylor, $60.19 for the deposition of Lawrence Bean and $160.75 for the deposition of David Weill. The entirely of the $182.75 sought in the costs memo for models, blowups and photocopies is taxed because no supporting documentation or information was provided for those costs. All other costs sought in the costs memo are allowed because they are permitted under the costs statutes and were reasonably incurred. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

Notice Of Motion To Tax Cost Sought By Defendant Paccar Inc
Off Calendar Jul-07-2011 Text Ruling

Motion To Tax Cost Sought By Defendant Arvinmeritor Inc
Off Calendar Jun-14-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Patrick M. Malone, Esq. of McKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE (415-267-4053), appearing for defendant / moving party ArvinMeritor Inc.
Carlos J.E. Guzman, Esq. of FARRISE LAW FIRM (800-748-6186), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Billy S. Johnson

RULING:
PLAINTIFF BILLY JOHNSON'S MOTION TO TAX COST SOUGHT BY DEFENDANT ARVINMERITOR INC.
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion to tax costs sought by defendant ArvinMeritor Inc. is granted in part and denied in part. All filing and motion fees claimed by defendant are taxed except $392 for the fees to file its answer and $522 for the fees to file its motion for summary judgment. Defendant's memorandum of costs and opposition papers contain insufficient information to enable the court to determine whether any of the other claimed filing and motion fees were 'reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation rather than merely convenient of beneficial.' (CCP 1033.5(c)(2)). All deposition costs claimed by defendant are taxed except $720.45 for volume 2 of James Shuman's deposition, $360.27 for volume 1 of James Shuman's deposition, $334.88 for volume 3 of James Shuman's deposition, $293.22 for volume 2 of the plaintiff's deposition, $164.97 for volume 1 of plaintiff's deposition and $213.77 for Thomas Nelson's deposition, The transcript of the summary judgment hearing is not a recoverable cost. Defendant's memorandum of costs and opposition papers contain insufficient information to enable the court to determine whether any of the other claimed deposition costs were 'reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation rather than merely convenient of beneficial.' All witness fees claimed by defendant are taxed. Defendant's memorandum of costs and opposition papers contain insufficient information to enable the court to determine whether any of the witness fees are recoverable under the costs statutes and, even if they are recoverable, whether they were 'reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation rather than merely convenient of beneficial.' All 'other' costs claimed by defendant are taxed. Defendant's memorandum of costs and opposition papers contain insufficient information to enable the court to determine whether any of these costs are recoverable under the costs statutes and, even if they are recoverable, whether they were 'reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation rather than merely convenient of beneficial.' Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

Jeremy D. Huie, Esq., of BASSI EDLIN HUIE & BLUM (415-403-4429), appearing for defendant Carlisle Corporation

RULING:
Argued and the Court rules as follows: DEFENDANT MAREMONT CORPORATION's Motion To Quash Plaintiff's Notice Of Deposition Of Person(S) Most Knowledgeable And Custodian Of Records And For Protective Order Regarding Plaintiff'S Deposition Notice, And For Monetary Sanctions Of $2,507 is denied. Maremont's Person Most Knowledgeable can be deposed for up to two hours no later than 2/2/11. There is no obligation for Maremont to provide to get this person up to speed other than providing the person with readily available documents with regards to the relationship between Maremont and its predecessor Grizzly, and Navistar and it's predecessors for the relevant time period 1965 to 1983. The 2-hour deposition is limited to the documents that were produced by Navistar from the Fort Wayne depository and the relationship between Navistar and Grizzly, for the 1965 to 1983 time period.

The Court sets a further case management conference on 2/9/11 at 1:30 PM in Department 220. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Jeannie M. Chimpky, CSR 971 (510-451-1580) = (220/HEK)

Jeremy D. Huie, Esq., of BASSI EDLIN HUIE & BLUM (415-403-4429), appearing for defendant / moving party Carlisle Corporation
Madeline Buty, Esq., of BUTY & CURLIANO (510-267-3000), appearing for defendant / opposing party Paccar, Inc,

RULING:
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion to augment expert witness disclosure is granted. Mr. Ferrari may be added to plaintiff's expert list and can testify at trial regarding the interpretation and meaning of documents received by plaintiff from the Fort Wayne depository provided that he possesses the requisite qualifications to do so. The determination of whether any opinions or other testimony offered by Mr. Ferrari are within the permissible scope of his testimony and whether Mr. Ferrari possesses the requisite qualifications to testify to those opinions and other testimony will be decided by the trial judge and are not being decided on this motion.
The parties stipulated to the following schedules: plaintiff is to produce Mr. Ferrari's file to defendants by end of the day on 1/20, Mr. Ferrari's deposition taken on 1/25 9:00 a.m., Defendants' further reply re: summary judgment motions filed by 4:30 p.m. 1/28 and courtesy copies also due by 4:30 p.m., summary judgment motions continued to 1/31/2011 at 1:30 p.m. (no tentative ruling issued), defendants to disclose rebuttal experts and produce their files by 2/7, experts deposed on 2/9-2/10, trial is continued to 2/14/2011 at 11:15 a.m. Plaintiff is to prepare an order. MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
James J. Ostertag, Esq. of NIXON PEABODY (415-984-8381), appearing for defendant / moving party Navistar, Inc.
Carlos J.E. Guzman, Esq. of FARRISE LAW FIRM (800-748-6186), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Billy Johnson

RULING:
Plaintiff's motion to compel the deposition of Navistar, Inc. Employee Tom Slavin
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion to compel the deposition of Mr. Slavin is granted in part. Plaintiff may take a deposition of Mr. Slavin at a location of Mr. Slavin's choice in the Chicago area not to exceed three hours which may take place over the course of two days if needed due to Mr. Slavin's health. The three hours excludes all speaking by counsel for Navistar other than less than ten word objections. The deposition may occur by telephone, videoconferencing or similar means. The deposition should conclude at the earliest possible date consistent with Mr. Slavin's health but no later than October 8. If at the conclusion of three hours plaintiff believes he needs additional time, the parties shall meet and confer and, if no agreement is reached, the court will entertain an ex parte application for additional time. Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Kim Richardson, CSR # 5915 (Tel: 510-451-1580)
